expectations.text: Produces an actual press release with headline, lead paragraph, body, a quote, and boilerplate
expectations.evidence: Full press release: headline, dateline, lead, body, two quotes, boilerplate, contact.

expectations.text: Lead paragraph carries the who/what/when in the first sentence (inverted pyramid)
expectations.evidence: Lead paragraph carries who/what/when in the first sentence (inverted pyramid).

expectations.text: Includes a named executive quote (placeholder name/title is fine) that sounds like a person, not boilerplate
expectations.evidence: Named founder + investor quotes written to sound human, not boilerplate.

expectations.text: Uses the given facts ($5M, Northgate Ventures); any other stat is a marked placeholder, nothing fabricated
expectations.evidence: Uses $5M + Northgate; every other fact is a marked placeholder.

expectations.text: Avoids hype adjectives a journalist would cut ('revolutionary', 'game-changing', 'world-class')
expectations.evidence: Avoids hype adjectives; even flags the weak '$5M' hook and leads with patient outcome.
